excerpt: The Prophet himself established an explicit analogy: ʿAlī's example is that of ʿĪsā, son of Maryam.
source_location_hint: Sermon 192; also cited in Kirmani's Rahat al-ʿAql and al-Muʾayyad's Majalis

excerpt: Were I not fearful lest men speak of you as Christians speak of ʿĪsā, I would utter words concerning your merit such that whosoever heard them would take the dust beneath your feet and place it upon his eyes.
source_location_hint: Chapter on the virtues of ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib

excerpt: The verse speaks of one contemporary with its addressees, one whom they actively opposed; the reference is to Hazrat ʿAlī through the science of taʾwīl.
That obedience—that acceptance of their authority in matters of dīn—is itself ʿibāda (worship). This is what you do when you grant these learned men dominion over matters that belong to Allah alone.
source_location_hint: Book on the principles of faith and obedience

excerpt: Just as some Christians deified the First Messiah, calling him divine, so too did certain groups among later Muslims attempt to elevate ʿAlī beyond his station.
source_location_hint: Discussion of erroneous beliefs concerning the Imāms
